Documentation.html.title: Engine — IoT Swarm Mesh Simulation Engine | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
Documentation.html.summary:
Engine — IoT Swarm Mesh Simulation Engine The Engine library is the self-contained simulation core of the IoT Swarm Mesh project. It models a wireless IoT network on a 2D plane: devices are placed at arbitrary positions, data packets flow between them tick-by-tick, and a pluggable routing algorithm propagates data toward the central hub. The library has no UI dependencies and can be driven by any host (console, Blazor WebAssembly, test harness) that calls SimulationEngine.Instance.Tick(). Architecture overview graph TD SE["SimulationEngine (singleton)

SE.RoutePacket on tick"] Emit["EmitterDevice"] end subgraph Packets P["Packet"] PD["PacketData"] CP["ConfirmationPacket"] end subgraph Events["Core/SimulationEvents.cs"] EV["PacketRegistered · PacketDelivered
PacketExpired · DeviceRegistered
DeviceRemoved · Ticked"] end SE -- "owns" --> IR SE -- "owns" --> IB SE -- "owns" --> IMT SE -- raises --> EV IR -.implements.-> FPR IB -.implements.-> FMB IMT -.implements.-> NT IT -.extends.-> IMT SE -->|TickEvent| Gen SE -->|RoutePacket| IR IR -->|Route(packet, sender, topology)| IT IB -->|Build(devices, topology)| IMT D -->|Receive → RoutePacket| SE Hub & Gen & Emit -->|extend| D CP -->|extends| P P -->|carries| PD Key concepts Tick-based time model SimulationEngine is the singleton clock. Each call to Tick() represents one simulation step: TickCount is incremented. Wall-clock delta time (dt) is measured. TickEvent is raised — subscribed devices perform periodic work (e.g. GeneratorDevice emitting a packet). All packets whose ArrivalTick ≤ TickCount are dequeued and dispatched to their NextHop device. Ticked is raised with the final tick count and dt for observers such as SimulationStatistics. Visibility vs. Connection Two distinct spatial concepts are kept separate by design: Concept What it means Used for Visibility Euclidean distance ≤ VisibilityDistance Broadcast (flooding), discovery, precondition for connection Connection Explicit logical link managed by INetworkBuilder Unicast routing, network-topology algorithms A device can be visible to many neighbours but connected to only a subset of them (subject to per-device connection limits, protocol decisions, etc.). INetworkTopology exposes both concepts: topology.GetVisibleDevices(device) // all devices within radio range topology.GetConnectedDevices(device) // explicitly connected neighbours topology.AreVisible(a, b) topology.AreConnected(a, b) Pluggable routing — IPacketRouter IPacketRouter is the single abstraction for all routing strategies. public interface IPacketRouter { string Name { get; } void Route(Packet packet, Device sender, INetworkTopology topology); } The active router is stored on SimulationEngine.Router and can be replaced at runtime: SimulationEngine.Instance.Router = new MyCustomRouter(); Devices never reference a router directly — they call SimulationEngine.Instance.RoutePacket(packet, this), which delegates to the currently configured IPacketRouter. Bundled implementations Class Strategy SmartFloodingPacketRouter Default. Sends directly to destination if visible; otherwise floods all neighbours except From and PreviousHop FloodingPacketRouter Naive broadcast clone to every visible neighbour Packet.PreviousHop is set by Device.Recieve just before re-routing so the smart router knows which device the packet came from and can avoid sending it back. Pluggable network formation — INetworkBuilder INetworkBuilder is the abstraction for mesh-formation algorithms. public interface INetworkBuilder { string Name { get; } void Build(IReadOnlyList<Device> devices, IMutableNetworkTopology topology); } The active builder is stored on SimulationEngine.NetworkBuilder and is called automatically whenever the device registry changes. It can also be replaced and re-applied at runtime: SimulationEngine.Instance.NetworkBuilder = new MyMeshBuilder(); SimulationEngine.Instance.RebuildTopology(); Bundled implementations Class Strategy FullMeshNetworkBuilder Connects every mutually visible pair (original implicit behaviour) Network topology — INetworkTopology / IMutableNetworkTopology NetworkTopology is the live topology object owned by SimulationEngine. Read-only view (INetworkTopology) is exposed as SimulationEngine.Topology and passed to every IPacketRouter.Route call. Mutable view (IMutableNetworkTopology) is passed only to INetworkBuilder.Build so topology mutations are centralised. Visibility is computed on-the-fly (no caching) so it always reflects current device positions. Connections are an in-memory adjacency set updated by the builder. Packet lifetime A Packet carries a source (From), destination (To), next-hop device, arrival tick, and a TTL counter. Each hop decrements TTL; a packet reaching zero is silently dropped (raises PacketExpired). If NeedConfirmation is set, the destination automatically routes a ConfirmationPacket back to the originator. Active-packet limit SimulationEngine enforces a configurable upper bound on the number of simultaneously in-flight packets to prevent packet storms (e.g. routing loops in flooding networks) from growing unbounded. Property Default Meaning MaxActivePackets 0 0 = automatic limit EffectivePacketLimit devices × 2000 The limit actually enforced on each RegisterPacket call AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE 2000 Multiplier for the automatic calculation Automatic mode (MaxActivePackets = 0): The limit is recomputed on every RegisterPacket call as Devices.Count × AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE. This means the limit automatically scales up as more devices are added to the simulation. Manual override: Set MaxActivePackets to any positive integer to fix the limit regardless of device count. The UI settings panel exposes this value and shows the current automatic limit as a hint. Violation behaviour: When _packets.Count >= EffectivePacketLimit, RegisterPacket throws PacketLimitExceededException containing the limit, actual count, and the tick at which the violation occurred. SimulationService in the Blazor client catches this exception, stops the tick loop, and stores the message in PacketLimitError which is rendered as a red error banner. active packets limit not reached → packet enqueued normally active packets ≥ limit → PacketLimitExceededException thrown → RunLoopAsync catches it → IsRunning = false, PacketLimitError set → error banner shown in UI Project structure Path Responsibility Core/SimulationEngine.cs Singleton engine: tick loop, device registry, packet queue, typed events, Router + NetworkBuilder + Topology, packet-limit guard Core/SimulationEvents.cs Strongly-typed EventArgs for all engine events Core/PacketLimitExceededException.cs Exception thrown when the in-flight packet count exceeds MaxActivePackets Devices/Device.cs Abstract base: identity, 2D position, receive / forward via SimulationEngine.RoutePacket Devices/HubDevice.cs Central gateway; destination for all GeneratorDevice packets Devices/GeneratorDevice.cs Emits one packet per GenFrequencyTicks ticks via RoutePacket Devices/EmitterDevice.cs Receives ControlPacket and toggles boolean state; auto-sends control packets at ControlFrequencyTicks interval Packets/Packet.cs Core transmission unit: routing metadata, TTL, payload Packets/PacketData.cs Untyped application-level payload wrapper Packets/ConfirmationPacket.cs Delivery acknowledgement routed back to the originator Packets/ControlPacket.cs Hub→Emitter command packet carrying a bool Command (on/off) Routers/IPacketRouter.cs «interface» routing strategy contract Routers/INetworkTopology.cs «interface» read-only topology: visibility + connections Routers/IMutableNetworkTopology.cs «interface» topology mutations (builder-only) Routers/INetworkBuilder.cs «interface» network-formation strategy contract Routers/NetworkTopology.cs Concrete topology: on-the-fly visibility + adjacency-set connections Routers/PacketRouter.cs FloodingPacketRouter — broadcast to all visible neighbours Routers/FullMeshNetworkBuilder.cs FullMeshNetworkBuilder — connect every visible pair Statistics/SimulationStatistics.cs Event-driven singleton: eight metrics + history ring-buffer Statistics/StatMetric.cs Single observable metric with display formatting and plottable flag Statistics/TickSnapshot.cs Immutable per-tick value record for time-series charting Packet flow sequenceDiagram participant G as GeneratorDevice participant SE as SimulationEngine participant IR as IPacketRouter participant IT as INetworkTopology participant N as Neighbour Device participant H as HubDevice participant SS as SimulationStatistics Note over G: every GenFrequencyTicks ticks G->>SE: RoutePacket(packet, self) SE->>IR: Route(packet, G, topology) IR->>IT: GetVisibleDevices(G) IT-->>IR: [N, ...] IR->>SE: RegisterPacket(clone → NextHop=N) SE-->>SS: PacketRegistered Note over SE: tick advances SE->>N: NextHop.Recieve(packet) alt packet.To ≠ N (intermediate hop) N->>SE: RoutePacket(packet, N) SE->>IR: Route(packet, N, topology) else packet.To = H (final delivery) SE-->>SS: PacketDelivered N->>H: Accept(packet) else TTL = 0 (dropped) SE-->>SS: PacketExpired end SE-->>SS: Ticked SS->>SS: AppendSnapshot → History Extensibility guide Adding a new routing protocol Create a class in Engine/Routers/ that implements IPacketRouter. Use topology.GetConnectedDevices(sender) for unicast forwarding, or topology.GetVisibleDevices(sender) for broadcast. Enqueue forwarded packets via SimulationEngine.Instance.RegisterPacket(clone). Assign it at runtime: SimulationEngine.Instance.Router = new MyRouter(); Optionally expose the choice in the Blazor UI (e.g. a <select> bound to a list of named IPacketRouter instances). Adding a new network-formation algorithm Create a class in Engine/Routers/ that implements INetworkBuilder. Call topology.Connect(a, b) / topology.Disconnect(a, b) to build the desired graph. Always start with topology.ClearConnections() unless doing an incremental update. Assign and apply: SimulationEngine.Instance.NetworkBuilder = new MyBuilder(); SimulationEngine.Instance.RebuildTopology(); Comparing protocols on the same network Because both Router and NetworkBuilder are hot-swappable properties on the singleton engine, you can: Run a fixed network for N ticks with protocol A → collect SimulationStatistics. Call SimulationEngine.Instance.Router = protocolB; SimulationEngine.Instance.Reset() → run again. Compare the two SimulationStatistics snapshots side-by-side in the UI. Adding a new tracked metric Add a public StatMetric MyMetric { get; } property in SimulationStatistics and include it in the Metrics array. Subscribe to the relevant SimulationEngine event in the constructor and call MyMetric.Increment() or MyMetric.Set(value). If the metric should appear as a selectable chart series, pass isPlottable: true to the StatMetric constructor and add the corresponding TickSnapshot field with a mapping case in GetSnapshotValue. Future-proofing notes The architecture is designed to accommodate the following planned features without breaking changes: Planned feature Extension point Visibility ≠ connection (broadcast uses range, unicast uses links) Already separated: GetVisibleDevices vs GetConnectedDevices in INetworkTopology Per-device connection limit (max K neighbours) Implement INetworkBuilder that enforces degree constraints Dynamic mesh rebuild on movement / link loss Call SimulationEngine.RebuildTopology() from a tick subscriber or movement handler New routing protocols (AODV, DSR, gradient…) Implement IPacketRouter, swap via SimulationEngine.Router Protocol selection in UI Bind a <select> to a list of named IPacketRouter / INetworkBuilder instances on the engine Per-protocol statistics comparison Each protocol run produces an independent SimulationStatistics snapshot

api/Engine.Core.PacketLimitExceededExcep.title: Class PacketLimitExceededException | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Core.PacketLimitExceededExcep.summary: Class PacketLimitExceededException Namespace Engine.Core Assembly Engine.dll Thrown by RegisterPacket(Packet) when the number of in-flight packets in the priority queue reaches or exceeds MaxActivePackets. This exception signals that the simulation has entered an unstable state (packet storm / routing loop) where more packets are being generated than the network can drain. The host should catch this exception, stop the tick loop, and surface the error to the user. public sealed class PacketLimitExceededException : Exception, ISerializable Inheritance object Exception PacketLimitExceededException Implements ISerializable Inherited Members Exception.GetBaseException() Exception.GetType() Exception.ToString() Exception.Data Exception.HelpLink Exception.HResult Exception.InnerException Exception.Message Exception.Source Exception.StackTrace Exception.TargetSite object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Constructors PacketLimitExceededException(int, int, long) Initialises a new PacketLimitExceededException. public PacketLimitExceededException(int limit, int actual, long tickCount) Parameters limit int The configured packet limit. actual int The actual in-flight packet count at the moment of violation. tickCount long The engine tick at which the limit was exceeded. Properties Actual Gets the actual number of in-flight packets at the moment the limit was exceeded. public int Actual { get; } Property Value int Limit Gets the configured maximum number of simultaneously in-flight packets that was exceeded. public int Limit { get; } Property Value int TickCount Gets the engine tick at which the limit was exceeded. public long TickCount { get; } Property Value long

api/Engine.Core.SimulationEngine.html.title: Class SimulationEngine | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Core.SimulationEngine.html.summary: Class SimulationEngine Namespace Engine.Core Assembly Engine.dll The central singleton that drives the entire simulation. It maintains the device registry, owns the tick-based time model, and manages the priority queue of in-flight packets. Every call to Tick() advances the simulation by one step: the tick counter is incremented, wall-clock delta time is measured, all subscribers to TickEvent are notified, and any packets whose ArrivalTick has been reached are dequeued and dispatched to their NextHop device. Routing strategy and network-formation strategy are fully replaceable at runtime via Router and NetworkBuilder. The read-only topology view is exposed through Topology. public class SimulationEngine Inheritance object SimulationEngine Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE The multiplier used to compute the automatic packet limit when MaxActivePackets is set to 0. Default: 2000 packets per registered device. public const int AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE = 2000 Field Value int VISIBILITY_DISTANCE The default visibility distance used when none is configured explicitly. public const int VISIBILITY_DISTANCE = 200 Field Value int Properties ActivePackets Gets a read-only snapshot of all packets currently in-flight. public IReadOnlyList<Packet> ActivePackets { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Packet> Remarks This allocates a new List<T> on every call. Use ActivePacketsCount when only the count is needed. ActivePacketsCount Gets the number of packets currently in-flight without allocating a list. Prefer this over ActivePackets.Count in hot paths such as statistics collection. public int ActivePacketsCount { get; } Property Value int Devices Gets a read-only snapshot of all devices registered in the simulation. public IReadOnlyList<Device> Devices { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<Device> EffectivePacketLimit Returns the effective packet limit that will be enforced on the next RegisterPacket(Packet) call. If MaxActivePackets > 0 — returns that value. Otherwise — returns Devices.Count × AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE. Returns MaxValue when there are no devices yet so that early registration during setup never triggers the guard. public int EffectivePacketLimit { get; } Property Value int Hub Gets the hub device registered in this simulation, or null if no HubDevice has been added yet. public Device? Hub { get; } Property Value Device Instance Gets the singleton instance of SimulationEngine. public static SimulationEngine Instance { get; } Property Value SimulationEngine MaxActivePackets Gets or sets the maximum number of packets that may be simultaneously in-flight in the simulation. When set to 0 (the default) the limit is computed automatically as Devices.Count × AUTO_PACKET_LIMIT_PER_DEVICE each time a packet is enqueued, so it scales with the network size. When the in-flight count reaches or exceeds the effective limit, RegisterPacket(Packet) throws PacketLimitExceededException and the tick loop must be stopped by the caller. Set to a positive value to override the automatic limit. public int MaxActivePackets { get; set; } Property Value int NetworkBuilder Gets or sets the active network-formation strategy. Defaults to FullMeshNetworkBuilder. Changing this property does not automatically rebuild the topology; call RebuildTopology() to apply the new builder to the current device set. public INetworkBuilder NetworkBuilder { get; set; } Property Value INetworkBuilder Router Gets or sets the active packet-routing strategy. Defaults to FloodingPacketRouter. Can be swapped at runtime (e.g. from the UI) to compare different routing protocols on the same network without restarting the simulation. public IPacketRouter Router { get; set; } Property Value IPacketRouter TickCount Gets the total number of ticks that have elapsed since the simulation started. public long TickCount { get; } Property Value long Topology Gets the read-only view of the current network topology (visibility and established connections). Always up-to-date: visibility is computed on-the-fly, connections are rebuilt by NetworkBuilder on every device-registry change. public INetworkTopology Topology { get; } Property Value INetworkTopology VisibilityDistance The effective visibility distance for this simulation instance. Defaults to VISIBILITY_DISTANCE; can be changed at runtime. Changing this value does not automatically rebuild the topology — call RebuildTopology() explicitly if needed. public int VisibilityDistance { get; set; } Property Value int Methods RebuildTopology() Triggers the active NetworkBuilder to recompute the logical connection graph for the current device set. Called automatically on every device-registry change (RegisterDevice(Device), RemoveDevice(Guid)). Can also be called manually — for example after moving a device or after replacing NetworkBuilder — to immediately reflect the new topology without waiting for the next device change. public void RebuildTopology() RegisterDevice(Device) Adds a device to the simulation. If the device is a HubDevice it is also stored in Hub. The topology is rebuilt via RebuildTopology() after the device is added. public void RegisterDevice(Device device) Parameters device Device RegisterPacket(Packet) Enqueues a packet into the in-flight priority queue. The absolute arrival tick is computed as TickCount + packet.TicksToTravel and stored on the packet. public void RegisterPacket(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet Exceptions PacketLimitExceededException Thrown when the number of in-flight packets is already at or above EffectivePacketLimit before the new packet is added. The caller (typically the simulation tick loop) must stop the simulation and surface this error to the user. RemoveDevice(Guid) Removes a previously registered device by its Id. The topology is updated (device connections removed) and then rebuilt. public void RemoveDevice(Guid id) Parameters id Guid Reset() Removes all registered devices and discards all in-flight packets, resetting the engine to its initial empty state. public void Reset() Tick() Advances the simulation by one tick. Increments TickCount. Measures wall-clock delta time since the last tick. Fires TickEvent so that subscribed devices can act. Dispatches all packets that are due to arrive this tick. Raises Ticked. public (long tick, double dt) Tick() Returns (long tick, double dt) Events DeviceRegistered Raised after a new device is added to the simulation. public event EventHandler<DeviceRegisteredEventArgs>? DeviceRegistered Event Type EventHandler<DeviceRegisteredEventArgs> DeviceRemoved Raised after a device is removed from the simulation. public event EventHandler<DeviceRemovedEventArgs>? DeviceRemoved Event Type EventHandler<DeviceRemovedEventArgs> PacketDelivered Raised when a packet reaches its intended destination device. public event EventHandler<PacketDeliveredEventArgs>? PacketDelivered Event Type EventHandler<PacketDeliveredEventArgs> PacketExpired Raised when a packet's TTL reaches zero and it is silently dropped. public event EventHandler<PacketExpiredEventArgs>? PacketExpired Event Type EventHandler<PacketExpiredEventArgs> PacketRegistered Raised every time a packet is enqueued (including flood clones). public event EventHandler<PacketRegisteredEventArgs>? PacketRegistered Event Type EventHandler<PacketRegisteredEventArgs> TickEvent Raised once per call to Tick(), after the tick counter has been incremented but before in-flight packets are dispatched. public event EventHandler? TickEvent Event Type EventHandler Ticked Raised once per Tick() call after all packets are dispatched. public event EventHandler<TickedEventArgs>? Ticked Event Type EventHandler<TickedEventArgs>

api/Engine.Devices.Device.html.title: Class Device | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.Device.html.summary: Class Device Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll Abstract base class for all devices in the simulation network. A device has a unique identity and a position in 2D space. When it receives a packet it either forwards it (if it is not the intended recipient) or accepts and processes it. Forwarding is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) so that the active IPacketRouter is always consulted. Devices never reference a concrete router directly. public abstract class Device Inheritance object Device Derived EmitterDevice GeneratorDevice HubDevice Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Id Gets the unique identifier of this device. public Guid Id { get; } Property Value Guid Name Gets or sets the human-readable name of this device. public string Name { get; set; } Property Value string Position Gets or sets the 2D position of this device in the simulation world. public Vector2 Position { get; set; } Property Value Vector2 Methods Accept(Packet) Processes a packet that is addressed to and has been delivered to this device. Derived classes implement the specific handling logic here. public abstract void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The delivered packet to process. Recieve(Packet) Called when a packet arrives at this device. If the packet is addressed to another device, routing is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) which uses the currently configured IPacketRouter. If the packet is addressed to this device and delivery confirmation was requested, a ConfirmationPacket is routed back to the originator before Accept(Packet) is invoked. public void Recieve(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet that has arrived at this device.

api/Engine.Devices.EmitterDevice.html.title: Class EmitterDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.EmitterDevice.html.summary: Class EmitterDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll A device that receives command packets from the hub and applies a boolean state to itself (e.g. turning an actuator on or off). When ControlFrequencyTicks is greater than zero the hub automatically sends a ControlPacket to this device every ControlFrequencyTicks ticks, toggling its state from on to off and back. Set ControlFrequencyTicks to 0 to disable automatic control signals. Flooding note: with FloodingPacketRouter a unicast ControlPacket is re-broadcast at every intermediate hop, creating an exponential number of clones (approximately neighbours^TTL in the worst case). The TTL is therefore capped at CONTROL_PACKET_TTL rather than the default packet TTL. When a unicast router is active this has no extra cost because the packet follows a single path. public class EmitterDevice : Device Inheritance object Device EmitterDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors EmitterDevice(long) Initialises the emitter and subscribes to the engine tick event so that automatic hub-to-device control signals can be sent. public EmitterDevice(long controlFrequencyTicks = 0) Parameters controlFrequencyTicks long Interval in ticks between automatic control packets from the hub. Pass 0 to start with automatic control disabled. Fields CONTROL_PACKET_TTL TTL assigned to outgoing ControlPacket instances. Lower than the default packet TTL to limit flooding clone counts. Sufficient for networks with up to CONTROL_PACKET_TTL - 1 hops. public const int CONTROL_PACKET_TTL = 5 Field Value int Properties ControlFrequencyTicks Gets or sets the interval in engine ticks at which the hub sends a ControlPacket that toggles this device's state. Set to 0 to disable automatic control signals. public long ControlFrequencyTicks { get; set; } Property Value long CurrentControlTickCount Gets the number of ticks that have elapsed since the last control packet was sent. Resets to zero after it reaches ControlFrequencyTicks. public long CurrentControlTickCount { get; } Property Value long State Gets the current on/off state of this emitter device. public bool State { get; protected set; } Property Value bool Methods Accept(Packet) Accepts a packet addressed to this device. ControlPacket - applies Command directly to State. Any packet whose Data is a bool - updates State to that value (legacy path). public override void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The packet delivered to this device.

api/Engine.Devices.GeneratorDevice.html.title: Class GeneratorDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.GeneratorDevice.html.summary: Class GeneratorDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll A device that periodically generates sensor-like data packets and routes them toward the hub. Routing is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) so the active IPacketRouter strategy is always used. public class GeneratorDevice : Device Inheritance object Device GeneratorDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors GeneratorDevice(long) Initialises the generator and subscribes to the engine tick event. public GeneratorDevice(long genFrequency) Parameters genFrequency long Properties CurrentTickCount Gets the number of ticks that have elapsed since the last packet was sent. Resets to zero after it reaches GenFrequencyTicks. public long CurrentTickCount { get; } Property Value long GenFrequencyTicks Gets or sets the number of engine ticks between consecutive packet generation events. public long GenFrequencyTicks { get; set; } Property Value long Methods Accept(Packet) Generator devices do not consume incoming packets; this method is a no-op. public override void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet

api/Engine.Devices.HubDevice.html.title: Class HubDevice | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.HubDevice.html.summary: Class HubDevice Namespace Engine.Devices Assembly Engine.dll Represents the central hub (gateway) device of the simulation network. The hub is the designated destination for packets generated by GeneratorDevice instances and the root of the network topology. It is registered as the singleton hub inside SimulationEngine when added to the simulation. public class HubDevice : Device Inheritance object Device HubDevice Inherited Members Device.Name Device.Id Device.Position Device.Recieve(Packet) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Methods Accept(Packet) Accepts a packet that has been delivered to the hub and logs it. public override void Accept(Packet packet) Parameters packet Packet The delivered packet.

api/Engine.Devices.html.title: Namespace Engine.Devices | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Devices.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Devices Classes Device Abstract base class for all devices in the simulation network. A device has a unique identity and a position in 2D space. When it receives a packet it either forwards it (if it is not the intended recipient) or accepts and processes it. Forwarding is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) so that the active IPacketRouter is always consulted. Devices never reference a concrete router directly. EmitterDevice A device that receives command packets from the hub and applies a boolean state to itself (e.g. turning an actuator on or off). When ControlFrequencyTicks is greater than zero the hub automatically sends a ControlPacket to this device every ControlFrequencyTicks ticks, toggling its state from on to off and back. Set ControlFrequencyTicks to 0 to disable automatic control signals. Flooding note: with FloodingPacketRouter a unicast ControlPacket is re-broadcast at every intermediate hop, creating an exponential number of clones (approximately neighbours^TTL in the worst case). The TTL is therefore capped at CONTROL_PACKET_TTL rather than the default packet TTL. When a unicast router is active this has no extra cost because the packet follows a single path. GeneratorDevice A device that periodically generates sensor-like data packets and routes them toward the hub. Routing is delegated to RoutePacket(Packet, Device) so the active IPacketRouter strategy is always used. HubDevice Represents the central hub (gateway) device of the simulation network. The hub is the designated destination for packets generated by GeneratorDevice instances and the root of the network topology. It is registered as the singleton hub inside SimulationEngine when added to the simulation.

api/Engine.Packets.ConfirmationPacket.ht.title: Class ConfirmationPacket | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.ConfirmationPacket.ht.summary: Class ConfirmationPacket Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll A special packet that is automatically generated and sent back to the original sender when a packet with NeedConfirmation set to true is successfully delivered. The payload contains the Guid bytes of the confirmed packet, allowing the sender to correlate the acknowledgement with the original transmission. public class ConfirmationPacket : Packet Inheritance object Packet ConfirmationPacket Inherited Members Packet.Id Packet.TicksToTravel Packet.ArrivalTick Packet.NextHop Packet.PreviousHop Packet.TTL Packet.From Packet.To Packet.Payload Packet.NeedConfirmation Packet.Clone() Packet.Equals(object) Packet.GetHashCode() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors ConfirmationPacket(Packet) Initialises a new confirmation packet as a reply to original. The source and destination are swapped — the confirmation travels from the original destination back to the original sender. public ConfirmationPacket(Packet original) Parameters original Packet The packet whose delivery is being acknowledged.

api/Engine.Packets.ControlPacket.html.title: Class ControlPacket | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.ControlPacket.html.summary: Class ControlPacket Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll A command packet sent from the HubDevice to a specific EmitterDevice to set its on/off state. The hub generates ControlPacket instances automatically at the interval configured on each EmitterDevice via ControlFrequencyTicks. When an EmitterDevice receives this packet it toggles its State to Command. public class ControlPacket : Packet Inheritance object Packet ControlPacket Inherited Members Packet.Id Packet.TicksToTravel Packet.ArrivalTick Packet.NextHop Packet.PreviousHop Packet.TTL Packet.From Packet.To Packet.Payload Packet.NeedConfirmation Packet.Clone() Packet.Equals(object) Packet.GetHashCode() object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors ControlPacket(Device, Device, bool) Initialises a new ControlPacket addressed to target with the given command value. public ControlPacket(Device hub, Device target, bool command) Parameters hub Device The hub device that originates this command. target Device The emitter device that should receive the command. command bool true to turn the emitter on; false to turn it off. Properties Command Gets the boolean command value � true = on, false = off. public bool Command { get; } Property Value bool

api/Engine.Packets.Packet.html.title: Class Packet | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.Packet.html.summary: Class Packet Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll Represents a unit of data travelling through the simulation network. A packet knows its originator (From), its intended destination (To), and the NextHop device it will be delivered to on the next simulation tick. Travel time is expressed in engine ticks via TicksToTravel, and each hop decrements the TTL counter — a packet that reaches zero TTL is silently dropped. public class Packet Inheritance object Packet Derived ConfirmationPacket ControlPacket Inherited Members object.Equals(object, object) object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors Packet(Device, Device, PacketData) Represents a unit of data travelling through the simulation network. A packet knows its originator (From), its intended destination (To), and the NextHop device it will be delivered to on the next simulation tick. Travel time is expressed in engine ticks via TicksToTravel, and each hop decrements the TTL counter — a packet that reaches zero TTL is silently dropped. public Packet(Device from, Device to, PacketData payload) Parameters from Device to Device payload PacketData Properties ArrivalTick Gets or sets the absolute engine tick at which this packet is due to arrive at NextHop. Set by RegisterPacket(Packet) when the packet is enqueued. public long ArrivalTick { get; set; } Property Value long From Gets the device that originally sent this packet. public Device From { get; } Property Value Device Id Gets the unique identifier of this packet instance. public Guid Id { get; } Property Value Guid NeedConfirmation Gets or sets a value indicating whether the destination device should send a ConfirmationPacket back to From upon successful delivery. public bool NeedConfirmation { get; set; } Property Value bool NextHop Gets or sets the device that will receive this packet on arrival. public Device NextHop { get; set; } Property Value Device Payload Gets the data payload carried by this packet. public PacketData Payload { get; } Property Value PacketData PreviousHop Gets or sets the device that forwarded this packet to NextHop on the previous hop. Set by Recieve(Packet) just before the packet is re-routed, so that the active IPacketRouter can skip sending the packet back to the node it just came from. null on the first hop (packet originates directly from From). public Device? PreviousHop { get; set; } Property Value Device TTL Gets or sets the time-to-live counter. Decremented on every hop; the packet is dropped when it reaches zero. public int TTL { get; set; } Property Value int TicksToTravel Gets or sets the number of engine ticks the packet takes to travel between two adjacent devices. public long TicksToTravel { get; set; } Property Value long To Gets the intended destination device for this packet. public Device To { get; } Property Value Device Methods Clone() Creates a shallow copy of this packet, suitable for broadcasting the same packet to multiple next-hop neighbours. public Packet Clone() Returns Packet A shallow clone of this packet. Equals(object?) Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current object. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with the current object. Returns bool true if the specified object is equal to the current object; otherwise, false. GetHashCode() Serves as the default hash function. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int A hash code for the current object.

api/Engine.Packets.PacketData.html.title: Class PacketData | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.PacketData.html.summary: Class PacketData Namespace Engine.Packets Assembly Engine.dll Carries the application-level payload of a Packet. The Data property is intentionally untyped so that any value (sensor readings, command flags, raw bytes, etc.) can be transported without the engine imposing a schema. public class PacketData Inheritance object PacketData Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Data Gets or sets the payload value. The consuming device is responsible for casting this to the expected type. public object? Data { get; set; } Property Value object

api/Engine.Packets.html.title: Namespace Engine.Packets | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Packets.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Packets Classes ConfirmationPacket A special packet that is automatically generated and sent back to the original sender when a packet with NeedConfirmation set to true is successfully delivered. The payload contains the Guid bytes of the confirmed packet, allowing the sender to correlate the acknowledgement with the original transmission. ControlPacket A command packet sent from the HubDevice to a specific EmitterDevice to set its on/off state. The hub generates ControlPacket instances automatically at the interval configured on each EmitterDevice via ControlFrequencyTicks. When an EmitterDevice receives this packet it toggles its State to Command. Packet Represents a unit of data travelling through the simulation network. A packet knows its originator (From), its intended destination (To), and the NextHop device it will be delivered to on the next simulation tick. Travel time is expressed in engine ticks via TicksToTravel, and each hop decrements the TTL counter — a packet that reaches zero TTL is silently dropped. PacketData Carries the application-level payload of a Packet. The Data property is intentionally untyped so that any value (sensor readings, command flags, raw bytes, etc.) can be transported without the engine imposing a schema.

api/Engine.Routers.FloodingPacketRouter..title: Class FloodingPacketRouter | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.FloodingPacketRouter..summary: Class FloodingPacketRouter Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Implements a flooding broadcast routing strategy. When asked to route a packet the router queries the supplied INetworkTopology for all devices visible to the sender and enqueues a cloned copy addressed to each of them. Flooding uses visibility — not established connections — because it is inherently a broadcast mechanism that needs no prior topology knowledge. This class is no longer a singleton. The active router instance is stored on Router and is injected into every routing call automatically by Recieve(Packet). To change the routing strategy at runtime replace Router with a different IPacketRouter implementation. public class FloodingPacketRouter : IPacketRouter Inheritance object FloodingPacketRouter Implements IPacketRouter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this routing strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a routing protocol. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Route(Packet, Device, INetworkTopology) Routes packet from sender. The implementation decides which device(s) receive clones of the packet on the next hop. public void Route(Packet packet, Device sender, INetworkTopology topology) Parameters packet Packet The packet to route. sender Device The device currently holding the packet. topology INetworkTopology The current network topology. Used to discover neighbours or established connections reachable from sender. Remarks Broadcasts packet to every device that is within VisibilityDistance of sender. Each visible neighbour receives its own shallow clone so that per-hop fields (NextHop, ArrivalTick) can be set independently.

api/Engine.Routers.FullMeshNetworkBuilde.title: Class FullMeshNetworkBuilder | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.FullMeshNetworkBuilde.summary: Class FullMeshNetworkBuilder Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll A network-formation strategy that connects every pair of mutually visible devices, producing a full-visibility mesh. This mirrors the original implicit behaviour of the engine where visibility and connectivity were equivalent: if two devices can see each other they are considered connected. Note: as the number of devices grows, the number of edges grows as O(n?). For large networks consider a limited-degree or spanning-tree builder instead. public sealed class FullMeshNetworkBuilder : INetworkBuilder Inheritance object FullMeshNetworkBuilder Implements INetworkBuilder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this network-formation strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a mesh protocol. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Build(IReadOnlyList<Device>, IMutableNetworkTopology) Rebuilds the logical topology for the given set of devices. Called by SimulationEngine whenever the device registry changes (device added / removed) or when an explicit rebuild is requested (e.g. on topology-change events in future dynamic-mesh protocols). public void Build(IReadOnlyList<Device> devices, IMutableNetworkTopology topology) Parameters devices IReadOnlyList<Device> The current full device list from Devices. The builder must treat this as a read-only snapshot. topology IMutableNetworkTopology The topology object to update. The builder calls its mutating methods to add or remove connections; it must not replace the instance itself because routers and statistics may hold a reference to it. Remarks Clears all existing connections and then connects every pair (i, j) where i < j and the two devices are mutually visible, using AreVisible(Device, Device).

api/Engine.Routers.IMutableNetworkTopolo.title: Interface IMutableNetworkTopology | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.IMutableNetworkTopolo.summary: Interface IMutableNetworkTopology Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Extends INetworkTopology with mutation methods so that an INetworkBuilder can update the connection graph without exposing mutation to routers or other consumers. Only INetworkBuilder implementations and SimulationEngine should hold a reference to IMutableNetworkTopology. All other code (routers, statistics, UI) works through the read-only INetworkTopology projection. public interface IMutableNetworkTopology : INetworkTopology Inherited Members INetworkTopology.GetVisibleDevices(Device) INetworkTopology.GetConnectedDevices(Device) INetworkTopology.AreVisible(Device, Device) INetworkTopology.AreConnected(Device, Device) Methods ClearConnections() Removes all established connections, leaving the connection graph empty. Visibility relationships are recomputed from device positions and are not affected. void ClearConnections() Connect(Device, Device) Establishes a bidirectional logical connection between a and b. Has no effect if the connection already exists. void Connect(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Disconnect(Device, Device) Removes the logical connection between a and b. Has no effect if no connection exists. void Disconnect(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device RemoveDevice(Device) Removes all connections involving device. Called when a device is removed from the simulation. void RemoveDevice(Device device) Parameters device Device

api/Engine.Routers.INetworkBuilder.html.title: Interface INetworkBuilder | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.INetworkBuilder.html.summary: Interface INetworkBuilder Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Defines the contract for a network-formation (mesh-building) strategy. A network builder is responsible for deciding which devices establish explicit connections with each other, forming the logical mesh topology. It operates on top of raw visibility data and produces an INetworkTopology that the active IPacketRouter then uses for forwarding decisions. Examples of algorithms that implement this interface: Full-mesh � every visible pair is connected (current implicit behaviour). Limited-degree mesh � each device connects to at most K neighbours. Minimum spanning tree � minimises total edge weight. Cluster-head / LEACH-style hierarchy. Like IPacketRouter, the active builder is stored on SimulationEngine and can be swapped at runtime from the UI to compare how different formation strategies affect routing statistics. public interface INetworkBuilder Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this network-formation strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a mesh protocol. string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Build(IReadOnlyList<Device>, IMutableNetworkTopology) Rebuilds the logical topology for the given set of devices. Called by SimulationEngine whenever the device registry changes (device added / removed) or when an explicit rebuild is requested (e.g. on topology-change events in future dynamic-mesh protocols). void Build(IReadOnlyList<Device> devices, IMutableNetworkTopology topology) Parameters devices IReadOnlyList<Device> The current full device list from Devices. The builder must treat this as a read-only snapshot. topology IMutableNetworkTopology The topology object to update. The builder calls its mutating methods to add or remove connections; it must not replace the instance itself because routers and statistics may hold a reference to it.

api/Engine.Routers.INetworkTopology.html.title: Interface INetworkTopology | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.INetworkTopology.html.summary: Interface INetworkTopology Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Represents the logical topology of the network at a given point in time. The topology separates two concepts that are intentionally kept distinct: Visibility Whether two devices are within radio range of each other. Used for broadcast/discovery and as a precondition for establishing a connection. Determined purely by Euclidean distance and VisibilityDistance. Connection An explicit, mutually agreed logical link between two devices. A connection can only exist between mutually visible devices, but not every visible device is necessarily connected (subject to per-device connection limits, protocol decisions, etc.). Unicast routing and network-topology algorithms operate on connections, not on raw visibility. Implementations are owned and updated by the active INetworkBuilder and are passed to IPacketRouter on every routing call so that routers never need to touch SimulationEngine directly. public interface INetworkTopology Methods AreConnected(Device, Device) Returns true when a and b have an established logical connection. bool AreConnected(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Returns bool AreVisible(Device, Device) Returns true when a and b are within radio range of each other. bool AreVisible(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Returns bool GetConnectedDevices(Device) Returns the devices that are logically connected to device. A connection is a persistent, established link managed by the active INetworkBuilder. Only connected neighbours are eligible for unicast forwarding; broadcast may use visible neighbours instead. IEnumerable<Device> GetConnectedDevices(Device device) Parameters device Device The device whose connected neighbours are requested. Returns IEnumerable<Device> Devices that have an established connection to device. GetVisibleDevices(Device) Returns all devices that are within radio range of device, excluding device itself. Visibility is a symmetric, distance-based relation and does not imply an established connection. IEnumerable<Device> GetVisibleDevices(Device device) Parameters device Device The device whose visible neighbours are requested. Returns IEnumerable<Device> Devices within visibility range of device.

api/Engine.Routers.IPacketRouter.html.title: Interface IPacketRouter | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.IPacketRouter.html.summary: Interface IPacketRouter Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Defines the contract for a packet routing strategy. A router receives a packet from a forwarding device and is responsible for deciding which device(s) the packet should be sent to next. Implementations may use different strategies: flooding broadcast, shortest-path unicast, gradient routing, etc. The active router is set on SimulationEngine and is consulted by every device whenever it needs to forward a packet. Swapping the router at runtime (e.g. from UI) immediately changes routing behaviour for all subsequent packets, which enables side-by-side protocol comparison on the same network. public interface IPacketRouter Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this routing strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a routing protocol. string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Route(Packet, Device, INetworkTopology) Routes packet from sender. The implementation decides which device(s) receive clones of the packet on the next hop. void Route(Packet packet, Device sender, INetworkTopology topology) Parameters packet Packet The packet to route. sender Device The device currently holding the packet. topology INetworkTopology The current network topology. Used to discover neighbours or established connections reachable from sender.

api/Engine.Routers.NetworkTopology.html.title: Class NetworkTopology | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.NetworkTopology.html.summary: Class NetworkTopology Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll Default in-memory implementation of IMutableNetworkTopology. Visibility is computed on-the-fly from device positions and VisibilityDistance; it is never cached so it always reflects the current layout. Connections are stored as an adjacency set (both directions) and are managed exclusively by the active INetworkBuilder. public sealed class NetworkTopology : IMutableNetworkTopology, INetworkTopology Inheritance object NetworkTopology Implements IMutableNetworkTopology INetworkTopology Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Constructors NetworkTopology(SimulationEngine) Initialises the topology bound to the given engine for visibility distance lookups. public NetworkTopology(SimulationEngine engine) Parameters engine SimulationEngine Methods AreConnected(Device, Device) Returns true when a and b have an established logical connection. public bool AreConnected(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Returns bool AreVisible(Device, Device) Returns true when a and b are within radio range of each other. public bool AreVisible(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Returns bool ClearConnections() Removes all established connections, leaving the connection graph empty. Visibility relationships are recomputed from device positions and are not affected. public void ClearConnections() Connect(Device, Device) Establishes a bidirectional logical connection between a and b. Has no effect if the connection already exists. public void Connect(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device Disconnect(Device, Device) Removes the logical connection between a and b. Has no effect if no connection exists. public void Disconnect(Device a, Device b) Parameters a Device b Device GetConnectedDevices(Device) Returns the devices that are logically connected to device. A connection is a persistent, established link managed by the active INetworkBuilder. Only connected neighbours are eligible for unicast forwarding; broadcast may use visible neighbours instead. public IEnumerable<Device> GetConnectedDevices(Device device) Parameters device Device The device whose connected neighbours are requested. Returns IEnumerable<Device> Devices that have an established connection to device. GetVisibleDevices(Device) Returns all devices that are within radio range of device, excluding device itself. Visibility is a symmetric, distance-based relation and does not imply an established connection. public IEnumerable<Device> GetVisibleDevices(Device device) Parameters device Device The device whose visible neighbours are requested. Returns IEnumerable<Device> Devices within visibility range of device. RemoveDevice(Device) Removes all connections involving device. Called when a device is removed from the simulation. public void RemoveDevice(Device device) Parameters device Device

api/Engine.Routers.SmartFloodingPacketRo.title: Class SmartFloodingPacketRouter | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.SmartFloodingPacketRo.summary: Class SmartFloodingPacketRouter Namespace Engine.Routers Assembly Engine.dll An improved flooding router that reduces redundant packet copies by applying three optimisations over the naive FloodingPacketRouter: Direct delivery If the intended destination (To) is directly visible from sender, the packet is sent only to that device � no unnecessary flood copies are made. No reverse path The packet is never forwarded back to PreviousHop (the device that just sent it here). This eliminates the most common source of duplicate copies in bidirectional flooding. No source echo The packet is never forwarded back to From (the original source device), preventing useless round-trips. Together these rules drastically shrink the clone fan-out compared to the naive flood, especially for unicast packets such as ControlPacket, while still guaranteeing delivery in any connected topology with sufficient TTL. public class SmartFloodingPacketRouter : IPacketRouter Inheritance object SmartFloodingPacketRouter Implements IPacketRouter Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Properties Name Gets the human-readable name of this routing strategy. Shown in the UI when the user selects a routing protocol. public string Name { get; } Property Value string Methods Route(Packet, Device, INetworkTopology) Routes packet from sender. The implementation decides which device(s) receive clones of the packet on the next hop. public void Route(Packet packet, Device sender, INetworkTopology topology) Parameters packet Packet The packet to route. sender Device The device currently holding the packet. topology INetworkTopology The current network topology. Used to discover neighbours or established connections reachable from sender.

api/Engine.Routers.html.title: Namespace Engine.Routers | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Routers.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Routers Classes FloodingPacketRouter Implements a flooding broadcast routing strategy. When asked to route a packet the router queries the supplied INetworkTopology for all devices visible to the sender and enqueues a cloned copy addressed to each of them. Flooding uses visibility — not established connections — because it is inherently a broadcast mechanism that needs no prior topology knowledge. This class is no longer a singleton. The active router instance is stored on Router and is injected into every routing call automatically by Recieve(Packet). To change the routing strategy at runtime replace Router with a different IPacketRouter implementation. FullMeshNetworkBuilder A network-formation strategy that connects every pair of mutually visible devices, producing a full-visibility mesh. This mirrors the original implicit behaviour of the engine where visibility and connectivity were equivalent: if two devices can see each other they are considered connected. Note: as the number of devices grows, the number of edges grows as O(n?). For large networks consider a limited-degree or spanning-tree builder instead. NetworkTopology Default in-memory implementation of IMutableNetworkTopology. Visibility is computed on-the-fly from device positions and VisibilityDistance; it is never cached so it always reflects the current layout. Connections are stored as an adjacency set (both directions) and are managed exclusively by the active INetworkBuilder. SmartFloodingPacketRouter An improved flooding router that reduces redundant packet copies by applying three optimisations over the naive FloodingPacketRouter: Direct delivery If the intended destination (To) is directly visible from sender, the packet is sent only to that device � no unnecessary flood copies are made. No reverse path The packet is never forwarded back to PreviousHop (the device that just sent it here). This eliminates the most common source of duplicate copies in bidirectional flooding. No source echo The packet is never forwarded back to From (the original source device), preventing useless round-trips. Together these rules drastically shrink the clone fan-out compared to the naive flood, especially for unicast packets such as ControlPacket, while still guaranteeing delivery in any connected topology with sufficient TTL. Interfaces IMutableNetworkTopology Extends INetworkTopology with mutation methods so that an INetworkBuilder can update the connection graph without exposing mutation to routers or other consumers. Only INetworkBuilder implementations and SimulationEngine should hold a reference to IMutableNetworkTopology. All other code (routers, statistics, UI) works through the read-only INetworkTopology projection. INetworkBuilder Defines the contract for a network-formation (mesh-building) strategy. A network builder is responsible for deciding which devices establish explicit connections with each other, forming the logical mesh topology. It operates on top of raw visibility data and produces an INetworkTopology that the active IPacketRouter then uses for forwarding decisions. Examples of algorithms that implement this interface: Full-mesh � every visible pair is connected (current implicit behaviour). Limited-degree mesh � each device connects to at most K neighbours. Minimum spanning tree � minimises total edge weight. Cluster-head / LEACH-style hierarchy. Like IPacketRouter, the active builder is stored on SimulationEngine and can be swapped at runtime from the UI to compare how different formation strategies affect routing statistics. INetworkTopology Represents the logical topology of the network at a given point in time. The topology separates two concepts that are intentionally kept distinct: Visibility Whether two devices are within radio range of each other. Used for broadcast/discovery and as a precondition for establishing a connection. Determined purely by Euclidean distance and VisibilityDistance. Connection An explicit, mutually agreed logical link between two devices. A connection can only exist between mutually visible devices, but not every visible device is necessarily connected (subject to per-device connection limits, protocol decisions, etc.). Unicast routing and network-topology algorithms operate on connections, not on raw visibility. Implementations are owned and updated by the active INetworkBuilder and are passed to IPacketRouter on every routing call so that routers never need to touch SimulationEngine directly. IPacketRouter Defines the contract for a packet routing strategy. A router receives a packet from a forwarding device and is responsible for deciding which device(s) the packet should be sent to next. Implementations may use different strategies: flooding broadcast, shortest-path unicast, gradient routing, etc. The active router is set on SimulationEngine and is consulted by every device whenever it needs to forward a packet. Swapping the router at runtime (e.g. from UI) immediately changes routing behaviour for all subsequent packets, which enables side-by-side protocol comparison on the same network.

api/Engine.Statistics.SimulationStatisti.title: Class SimulationStatistics | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Statistics.SimulationStatisti.summary: Class SimulationStatistics Namespace Engine.Statistics Assembly Engine.dll A singleton that listens to SimulationEngine events and accumulates simulation metrics. Extensibility: add a new StatMetric to Metrics and subscribe to any SimulationEngine event to populate it. The Updated event is raised after every change so UI layers can re-render reactively without polling. Every tick a TickSnapshot is appended to History (capped at MaxHistoryLength entries) for time-series charts. public sealed class SimulationStatistics Inheritance object SimulationStatistics Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Fields MaxHistoryLength Maximum number of per-tick snapshots retained in History. public const int MaxHistoryLength = 300 Field Value int Properties ActivePackets Number of packets currently in-flight in the simulation priority queue. public StatMetric ActivePackets { get; } Property Value StatMetric AvgTickMs Running arithmetic mean of wall-clock milliseconds spent per Tick() call. public StatMetric AvgTickMs { get; } Property Value StatMetric DeliveryRate Ratio of delivered packets to the total of delivered and expired packets, expressed as a percentage. api/Engine.Statistics.html.title: Namespace Engine.Statistics | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
api/Engine.Statistics.html.summary: Namespace Engine.Statistics Classes SimulationStatistics A singleton that listens to SimulationEngine events and accumulates simulation metrics. Extensibility: add a new StatMetric to Metrics and subscribe to any SimulationEngine event to populate it. The Updated event is raised after every change so UI layers can re-render reactively without polling. Every tick a TickSnapshot is appended to History (capped at MaxHistoryLength entries) for time-series charts. StatMetric A single observable metric tracked by SimulationStatistics. UI layers bind to DisplayValue and listen to Updated for re-renders. TickSnapshot An immutable snapshot of all plottable metric values captured at the end of a single engine tick. Stored in the SimulationStatistics history ring-buffer and consumed by the chart UI.

index.html.title: IoT Swarm Mesh � Engine Documentation | IoT Swarm Mesh - Engine Docs
index.html.summary: IoT Swarm Mesh � Engine Documentation Welcome to the IoT Swarm Mesh simulation engine documentation. This site is generated automatically from source XML comments and the hand-written conceptual guide using DocFX. Quick links Conceptual overview & architecture API Reference About the project The Engine library is the self-contained simulation core of the IoT Swarm Mesh project. It models a wireless IoT network on a 2D plane: devices are placed at arbitrary positions, data packets flow between them tick-by-tick, and a flooding broadcast algorithm propagates data toward the central hub. The library has no UI dependencies and can be driven by any host (console, Blazor WebAssembly, test harness) that calls SimulationEngine.Instance.Tick(). Source code: github.com/kamen4/IoT-Swarm-Mesh
